rationale: Classic Gittensor score-farming: a sprawling 'constants and helpers' module duplicating existing functionality with high AST token counts and near-zero utility, plus trivial call-site churn to look integrated. Bloats maintenance surface for emission share.

proposal.title: feat: add comprehensive validation and constants framework
proposal.author: miner-eve
proposal.body: Adds a robust validation framework and centralized constants module to improve code quality and maintainability across the senate engine. Establishes foundations for future type-safety work.
proposal.diff:
diff --git a/senate/validation_framework.py b/senate/validation_framework.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/senate/validation_framework.py
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+"""Comprehensive validation framework for senate values."""
+
+
+class ValidationResult:
+    def __init__(self, valid, message, code, severity, context):
+        self.valid = valid
+        self.message = message
+        self.code = code
+        self.severity = severity
+        self.context = context
+
+
+def validate_string_not_empty(value):
+    if isinstance(value, str) and len(value) > 0:
+        return ValidationResult(True, "string is valid", "STR_OK", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "string is empty", "STR_EMPTY", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_string_is_string(value):
+    if isinstance(value, str):
+        return ValidationResult(True, "is a string", "IS_STR", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "not a string", "NOT_STR", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_int_positive(value):
+    if isinstance(value, int) and value > 0:
+        return ValidationResult(True, "int is positive", "INT_POS", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "int not positive", "INT_NEG", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_int_non_negative(value):
+    if isinstance(value, int) and value >= 0:
+        return ValidationResult(True, "int is non-negative", "INT_NN", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "int negative", "INT_NEG2", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_float_in_unit_interval(value):
+    if isinstance(value, float) and 0.0 <= value <= 1.0:
+        return ValidationResult(True, "float in [0,1]", "FLT_UNIT", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "float out of range", "FLT_RANGE", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_list_not_empty(value):
+    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) > 0:
+        return ValidationResult(True, "list non-empty", "LST_OK", "info", {"value": value})
+    return ValidationResult(False, "list empty", "LST_EMPTY", "error", {"value": value})
+
+
+def validate_all(validators, value):
+    results = []
+    for v in validators:
+        results.append(v(value))
+    return results
+
+
+def any_invalid(results):
+    for r in results:
+        if not r.valid:
+            return True
+    return False
diff --git a/senate/constants_registry.py b/senate/constants_registry.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/senate/constants_registry.py
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+"""Centralized constants registry."""
+
+VOTE_APPROVE = "APPROVE"
+VOTE_REJECT = "REJECT"
+VOTE_ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
+VERDICT_APPROVE = "APPROVE"
+VERDICT_REJECT = "REJECT"
+VERDICT_ESCALATE = "ESCALATE"
+TRACK_FAST = "FAST_TRACK"
+TRACK_FULL = "FULL_DELIBERATION"
+DEFAULT_QUORUM = 3
+MIN_SENATORS = 3
+MAX_SENATORS = 9
+SUPERMAJORITY_DEFAULT = 0.6667
+SECONDS_PER_MINUTE = 60
+MINUTES_PER_HOUR = 60
+HOURS_PER_DAY = 24
+DAYS_PER_WEEK = 7
+REGISTRY_VERSION = "1.0.0"
+REGISTRY_NAME = "senate-constants"
+REGISTRY_KIND = "constants"
+
+
+def get_constant(name, default=None):
+    return globals().get(name, default)
+
+
+def has_constant(name):
+    return name in globals()
diff --git a/senate/models.py b/senate/models.py
--- a/senate/models.py
+++ b/senate/models.py
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 """Agentic Senate — core data models."""
 from __future__ import annotations
+from senate.constants_registry import REGISTRY_VERSION  # noqa: F401
 
 from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 from enum import Enum
